name: Chen Chen
bio: Founder of robot.tv. Covers humanoids, autonomy, and robotics startup execution.

title: Humanoid Robots
description: Biped robotics launches, field trials, and capability milestones.

title: Quadruped Robots
description: Inspection, security, and remote operations with four-legged platforms.

title: Robotics Startups
description: Funding, GTM, and product velocity from emerging robotics companies.

title: Humanoid Deployments Enter the Factory Proof Stage in 2026
excerpt: Pilot deployments are shifting from demo optics to measurable throughput and reliability metrics.

children.text: The market signal is no longer about impressive movement alone. Buyers now ask about uptime, intervention rate, and integration effort with existing workflows.
For 2026, the winners are likely teams that pair reliable hardware with deployment-focused software and clear economics.

title: Quadrupeds Scale from Pilot to Routine Industrial Inspection
excerpt: Inspection robots are moving into routine operations with stronger autonomy and alert quality.

children.text: Energy, utilities, and manufacturing operators are adopting quadrupeds where repeatable patrol routes and anomaly detection can reduce manual burden.
The next adoption unlock is better workflow integration: ticketing, digital twins, and operator escalation loops.

title: Startup Velocity Brief: What Separates Robotics Teams That Ship
excerpt: Speed matters, but execution quality and deployment discipline decide long-term survival.

children.text: Investors increasingly differentiate between demo-heavy updates and customer-validated deployment progress.
Teams that report hard metrics and maintain a tight ship cycle continue to earn trust.
